    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./README.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 96
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 141
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "The current release focuses on the core supervised learning components. RL post-training and route conditioning are potential candidates for future releases. Stay tuned!",
            "lineNumber": 98
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "text": "## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)",
            "lineNumber": 100
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;details&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 102
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;summary&gt;&lt;strong&gt;Does the 10B model accept navigation/route inputs?&lt;/strong&gt;&lt;/summary&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 103
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104
          },
          {
            "text": "While we have experimented with route conditioning capabilities, the released model does **not** include this feature. The current release takes multi-camera video and egomotion history as inputs, without explicit navigation or route inputs (e.g., waypoints, turn-by-turn navigation instructions).",
            "lineNumber": 105
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/details&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 107
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 108
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;details&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 109
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;summary&gt;&lt;strong&gt;Does the model produce meta-actions or support general VQA?&lt;/strong&gt;&lt;/summary&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 110
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111
          },
          {
            "text": "While we have experimented with meta-action and general VQA capabilities, the released model does **not** include these features. Alpamayo 1 is designed specifically for trajectory prediction with Chain-of-Causation reasoning, producing trajectory + reasoning trace outputs.",
            "lineNumber": 112
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/details&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 114
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;details&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 116
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;summary&gt;&lt;strong&gt;Was the 10B model post-trained with Reinforcement Learning (RL)?&lt;/strong&gt;&lt;/summary&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 117
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "text": "No. The current 10B model release has **not** undergone RL post-training. While the paper describes RL stages for improving reasoning quality and action consistency, this release focuses on the supervised learning components. As mentioned above, we may release RL post-trained models in future releases.",
            "lineNumber": 119
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/details&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 121
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;details&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 123
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;summary&gt;&lt;strong&gt;What are the minimum GPU requirements?&lt;/strong&gt;&lt;/summary&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 124
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 125
          },
          {
            "text": "You need an NVIDIA GPU with at least **24 GB VRAM** for inference. Tested configurations include RTX 3090, A100, and H100. Running on GPUs with less memory (e.g., 16 GB) will likely result in CUDA out-of-memory errors.",
            "lineNumber": 126
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 127
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/details&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 128
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 129
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;details&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 130
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;summary&gt;&lt;strong&gt;Can I use this model in production / commercial applications?&lt;/strong&gt;&lt;/summary&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 131
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 132
          },
          {
            "text": "No. The model weights are released under a **non-commercial license**. This release is intended for research, experimentation, and evaluation purposes only. See the [License](#license) section and the [HuggingFace Model Card](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/Alpamayo-R1-10B) for details.",
            "lineNumber": 133
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 134
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/details&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 135
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 136
          },
          {
            "text": "## Project Structure",
            "lineNumber": 137
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 138
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 139
          },
          {
            "text": "alpamayo/",
            "lineNumber": 140
          },
          {
            "text": "├── notebook/",
            "lineNumber": 141
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.2456957995891571
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/alpamayo_r1/models/delta_tokenizer.py",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 20
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 153
          }
        },
        "signatures": {
          "ranges": [
            {
              "startPosition": {
                "line": 21,
                "column": 1
              },
              "endPosition": {
                "line": 22,
                "column": 5
              }
            }
          ]
        },
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "class DeltaTrajectoryTokenizer:",
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "@property",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "def vocab_size(self) -&gt; int:",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "        return self.num_bins",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "    def encode(",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "        self,",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "        hist_xyz: torch.Tensor,",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "        hist_rot: torch.Tensor,",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "        fut_xyz: torch.Tensor,",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "        fut_rot: torch.Tensor,",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "        hist_tstamp: torch.Tensor | None = None,",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "        fut_tstamp: torch.Tensor | None = None,",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "text": "    ) -&gt; torch.LongTensor:",
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"\"\"Encodes the trajectories as discrete tokens. The model conditions on the historical",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "        waypoints to tokenize the future waypoints. Trajectories can be provided in any coordinate",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "        frame. Timestamps can be provided with any time-origin.",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "        Args:",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "text": "            hist_xyz (torch.Tensor): Historical locations XYZ. Shape: (B, Th, 3).",
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "text": "            hist_rot (torch.Tensor): Historical rotations. Shape: (B, Th, 3, 3).",
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "text": "            fut_xyz (torch.Tensor): Future locations XYZ. Shape: (B, Tf, 3).",
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "text": "            fut_rot (torch.Tensor): Future rotations. Shape: (B, Tf, 3, 3).",
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "text": "            hist_tstamp (torch.Tensor): Historical time stamps. Shape: (B, Th).",
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "text": "            fut_tstamp (torch.Tensor): Future time stamps. Shape: (B, Tf).",
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "text": "        Returns:",
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "text": "            torch.LongTensor: The token indices. Shape: (B, num_tokens_per_trajectory).",
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "text": "        del hist_xyz, hist_rot, hist_tstamp, fut_tstamp",
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "text": "        xyz = torch.nn.functional.pad(fut_xyz, [0, 0, 1, 0, 0, 0])",
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "text": "        xyz = xyz[:, 1:] - xyz[:, :-1]",
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "text": "        ego_xyz_max = torch.tensor(self.ego_xyz_max, dtype=xyz.dtype, device=xyz.device)",
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "text": "        ego_xyz_min = torch.tensor(self.ego_xyz_min, dtype=xyz.dtype, device=xyz.device)",
            "lineNumber": 75
          },
          {
            "text": "        xyz = (xyz - ego_xyz_min) / (ego_xyz_max - ego_xyz_min)",
            "lineNumber": 76
          },
          {
            "text": "        xyz = (xyz * (self.num_bins - 1)).round().long()",
            "lineNumber": 77
          },
          {
            "text": "        xyz = xyz.clamp(0, self.num_bins - 1)",
            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "text": "        if not self._predict_yaw:",
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "text": "            return einops.rearrange(xyz, \"b n m -&gt; b (n m)\")",
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "text": "        # Extract yaw angles from rotation matrices",
            "lineNumber": 81
          },
          {
            "text": "        yaw = torch.atan2(fut_rot[..., 0, 1], fut_rot[..., 0, 0])",
            "lineNumber": 82
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "text": "        # Calculate delta yaw",
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "text": "        yaw_padded = torch.nn.functional.pad(yaw, [1, 0, 0, 0])",
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "text": "        delta_yaw = yaw_padded[:, 1:] - yaw_padded[:, :-1]",
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "text": "        # Normalize delta yaw to [-pi, pi]",
            "lineNumber": 88
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.24423089623451233
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/alpamayo_r1/models/action_in_proj.py",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 69,
            "column": 28
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 165
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "class FourierEncoderV2(nn.Module):",
            "lineNumber": 73,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Improved Fourier feature encoder with logarithmically-spaced frequencies.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75
          },
          {
            "text": "    def __init__(self, dim: int, max_freq: float = 100.0):",
            "lineNumber": 76
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"\"\"Initialize the Fourier encoder V2.",
            "lineNumber": 77
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "text": "        Args:",
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "text": "            dim: Output dimension of the encoder. Must be even as it's split into",
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "text": "                sine and cosine components.",
            "lineNumber": 81
          },
          {
            "text": "            max_freq: Maximum frequency for the logarithmic frequency spacing.",
            "lineNumber": 82
          },
          {
            "text": "                Defaults to 100.0.",
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "text": "        super().__init__()",
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "text": "        half = dim // 2",
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "text": "        freqs = torch.logspace(0, math.log10(max_freq), steps=half)",
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "text": "        self.out_dim = dim",
            "lineNumber": 88
          },
          {
            "text": "        self.register_buffer(\"freqs\", freqs[None, :])  # (1, half)",
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90
          },
          {
            "text": "    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -&gt; torch.Tensor:",
            "lineNumber": 91
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"\"\"Forward pass of the Fourier encoder V2.",
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93
          },
          {
            "text": "        Args:",
            "lineNumber": 94
          },
          {
            "text": "            x: Input tensor of arbitrary shape (..., ).",
            "lineNumber": 95
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96
          },
          {
            "text": "        Returns:",
            "lineNumber": 97
          },
          {
            "text": "            Fourier-encoded features of shape (..., dim).",
            "lineNumber": 98
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "text": "        arg = x[..., None] * self.freqs * 2 * torch.pi  # (*, half_dim)",
            "lineNumber": 100
          },
          {
            "text": "        return torch.cat([torch.sin(arg), torch.cos(arg)], -1) * math.sqrt(2)",
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103
          },
          {
            "text": "class PerWaypointActionInProjV2(torch.nn.Module):",
            "lineNumber": 104,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 105
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.23804689943790436
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./README.md",
        "range": {

          {
            "text": "No. The model weights are released under a **non-commercial license**. This release is intended for research, experimentation, and evaluation purposes only.           },
          {
            "lineNumber": 134
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/details&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 135
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 136
          },
          {
            "text": "## Project Structure",
            "lineNumber": 137
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 138
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",

          },
          {

          },

          },
          {

          },

          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 160
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 161
          },
          {
            "text": "## Troubleshooting",
            "lineNumber": 162
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 163
          },
          {
            "text": "### Flash Attention issues",
            "lineNumber": 164
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 165
          },
          {
            "text": "The model uses Flash Attention 2 by default. If you encounter compatibility issues:",
            "lineNumber": 166
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 167
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;python",
            "lineNumber": 168
          },
          {
            "text": "# Use PyTorch's scaled dot-product attention instead",
            "lineNumber": 169
          },
          {
            "text": "config.attn_implementation = \"sdpa\"",
            "lineNumber": 170
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 171
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 172
          },
          {
            "text": "### CUDA out-of-memory errors",
            "lineNumber": 173
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 174
          },
          {
            "text": "If you encounter OOM errors:",
            "lineNumber": 175
          },
          {
            "text": "1. Ensure you have a GPU with at least 24 GB VRAM",
            "lineNumber": 176
          },
          {
            "text": "2. Reduce &#96;num_traj_samples&#96; if generating multiple trajectories",
            "lineNumber": 177
          },
          {
            "text": "3. Close other GPU-intensive applications",
            "lineNumber": 178
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 179
          },
          {
            "text": "## License",
            "lineNumber": 180
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 181
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Inference code**: Apache License 2.0 - see [LICENSE](./LICENSE) for details.",
            "lineNumber": 182
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Model weights**: Non-commercial license - see [HuggingFace Model Card](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/Alpamayo-R1-10B) for details.",
            "lineNumber": 183
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 184
          },
          {
            "text": "## Disclaimer",
            "lineNumber": 185
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 186
          },
          {
            "text": "Alpamayo 1 is a pre-trained reasoning model designed to accelerate research and development in the autonomous vehicle (AV) domain. It is intended to serve as a foundation for a range of AV-related use cases-from instantiating an end-to-end backbone for autonomous driving to enabling reasoning-based auto-labeling tools. In short, it should be viewed as a building block for developing customized AV applications.",
            "lineNumber": 187
          }
        ]
      },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/alpamayo_r1/models/action_in_proj.py",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 103
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 165,
            "column": 73
          }
        },
        "signatures": {
          "ranges": [
            {
              "startPosition": {
                "line": 104,

              },
              "endPosition": {
                "line": 105,

              }
            }
          ]
        },
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "class PerWaypointActionInProjV2(torch.nn.Module):",
            "lineNumber": 104,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 146
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 147
          },
          {
            "text": "    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, timesteps: torch.Tensor) -&gt; torch.Tensor:",
            "lineNumber": 148
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"\"\"Forward pass of the per-waypoint action projection V2.",
            "lineNumber": 149
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 150
          },
          {
            "text": "        Args:",
            "lineNumber": 151
          },
          {
            "text": "            x: Action tensor of shape (batch_size, num_waypoints, action_dim).",
            "lineNumber": 152
          },
          {
            "text": "            timesteps: Timestep tensor of shape (batch_size, ...). The last dimension",
            "lineNumber": 153
          },
          {
            "text": "                is used for encoding.",
            "lineNumber": 154
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 155
          },
          {
            "text": "        Returns:",
            "lineNumber": 156
          },
          {
            "text": "            Normalized projected action features of shape",
            "lineNumber": 157
          },
          {
            "text": "            (batch_size, num_waypoints, out_dim).",
            "lineNumber": 158
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 159
          },
          {
            "text": "        B, T, _ = x.shape",
            "lineNumber": 160
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 161
          },
          {
            "text": "        action_feats = torch.cat([s(x[:, :, i]) for i, s in enumerate(self.sinus)], dim=-1)",
            "lineNumber": 162
          },
          {
            "text": "        timestep_feats = self.timestep_fourier_encoder(timesteps[..., -1])",
            "lineNumber": 163
          },
          {
            "text": "        timestep_feats = timestep_feats.repeat(1, T, 1)",
            "lineNumber": 164
          },
          {
            "text": "        x = torch.cat((action_feats, timestep_feats), dim=-1)",
            "lineNumber": 165
          },
          {
            "text": "        return self.norm(self.encoder(x.flatten(0, 1)).reshape(B, T, -1))",
            "lineNumber": 166,
            "isSignature": true
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.2344733476638794
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.specstory/history/2026-03-11_04-52Z-model-input-and-output-analysis.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 1489
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1548
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "with open('/data/dataset/nuscenes/v1.0-trainval/v1.0-trainval/sample.json') as f:",
            "lineNumber": 1490
          },
          {
            "text": "    samples = json.load(f)",
            "lineNumber": 1491
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1492
          },
          {
            "text": "# Build sample_data map",
            "lineNumber": 1493
          },
          {
            "text": "sd_map = {d['token']: d for d in sample_data}",
            "lineNumber": 1494
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1495
          },
          {
            "text": "# Get first scene's first sample",
            "lineNumber": 1496
          },
          {
            "text": "scene0_first_sample = samples[0]['token']",
            "lineNumber": 1497
          },
          {
            "text": "# Find the LIDAR_TOP sample_data for this sample",
            "lineNumber": 1498
          },
          {
            "text": "lidar_sd = [d for d in sample_data if d['sample_token'] == scene0_first_sample and 'LIDAR_TOP' in d['filename'] and d['is_key_frame']]",
            "lineNumber": 1499
          },
          {
            "text": "if lidar_sd:",
            "lineNumber": 1500
          },
          {
            "text": "    sd = lidar_sd[0]",
            "lineNumber": 1501
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Walk through the linked list",
            "lineNumber": 1502
          },
          {
            "text": "    timestamps = []",
            "lineNumber": 1503
          },
          {
            "text": "    count = 0",
            "lineNumber": 1504
          },
          {
            "text": "    current = sd['token']",
            "lineNumber": 1505
          },
          {
            "text": "    # go to the beginning",
            "lineNumber": 1506
          },
          {
            "text": "    while sd_map[current]['prev']:",
            "lineNumber": 1507
          },
          {
            "text": "        current = sd_map[current]['prev']",
            "lineNumber": 1508
          },
          {
            "text": "    # now walk forward",
            "lineNumber": 1509
          },
          {
            "text": "    while current and count &lt; 500:",
            "lineNumber": 1510
          },
          {
            "text": "        entry = sd_map[current]",
            "lineNumber": 1511
          },
          {
            "text": "        timestamps.append(entry['timestamp'])",
            "lineNumber": 1512
          },
          {
            "text": "        current = entry['next'] if entry['next'] else None",
            "lineNumber": 1513
          },
          {
            "text": "        count += 1",
            "lineNumber": 1514
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 1515
          },
          {
            "text": "    print(f'LIDAR_TOP entries (samples+sweeps) in first scene: {len(timestamps)}')",
            "lineNumber": 1516
          },
          {
            "text": "    print(f'Duration: {(timestamps[-1]-timestamps[0])/1e6:.1f}s')",
            "lineNumber": 1517
          },
          {
            "text": "    print(f'Frequency: {len(timestamps)/((timestamps[-1]-timestamps[0])/1e6):.1f} Hz')",
            "lineNumber": 1518
          },
          {
            "text": "\"",
            "lineNumber": 1519
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 1520
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1521
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 1522
          },
          {
            "text": "LIDAR_TOP entries (samples+sweeps) in first scene: 388",
            "lineNumber": 1523
          },
          {
            "text": "Duration: 19.5s",
            "lineNumber": 1524
          },
          {
            "text": "Frequency: 19.9 Hz",
            "lineNumber": 1525
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1526
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 1527
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/details&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 1528
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/tool-use&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 1529
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1530
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 1531
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1532
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;think&gt;&lt;details&gt;&lt;summary&gt;Thought Process&lt;/summary&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 1533
          },
          {
            "text": "Now I have a thorough understanding of both datasets. Let me now create the plan.",
            "lineNumber": 1534
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1535
          },
          {
            "text": "Key findings:",
            "lineNumber": 1536
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1537
          },
          {
            "text": "## Current Model Input/Output Analysis",
            "lineNumber": 1538
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1539
          },
          {
            "text": "### Input (from &#96;physical_ai_av&#96; dataset):",
            "lineNumber": 1540
          },
          {
            "text": "1. **image_frames**: &#96;(N_cameras, num_frames, 3, H, W)&#96; - 4 cameras × 4 frames each",
            "lineNumber": 1541
          },
          {
            "text": "   - Cameras: CAMERA_CROSS_LEFT_120FOV (idx 0), CAMERA_FRONT_WIDE_120FOV (idx 1), CAMERA_CROSS_RIGHT_120FOV (idx 2), CAMERA_FRONT_TELE_30FOV (idx 6)",
            "lineNumber": 1542
          },
          {
            "text": "   - Frames spanning from t0-0.3s through t0 at 10 Hz sampling",
            "lineNumber": 1543
          },
          {
            "text": "2. **camera_indices**: &#96;(N_cameras,)&#96; - sorted camera indices [0, 1, 2, 6]",
            "lineNumber": 1544
          },
          {
            "text": "3. **ego_history_xyz**: &#96;(1, 1, 16, 3)&#96; - 16 historical ego positions in local coordinates at 10 Hz (1.6s window)",
            "lineNumber": 1545
          },
          {
            "text": "4. **ego_history_rot**: &#96;(1, 1, 16, 3, 3)&#96; - corresponding rotation matrices for the ego vehicle's orientation history",
            "lineNumber": 1546
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1547
          },
          {
            "text": "The model then outputs predicted trajectories with 64 future timesteps: predicted positions and rotations across multiple trajectory samples, along with relative and absolute timestamp information for each camera.",
            "lineNumber": 1548
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.22651590406894684
    }

}

---

# Alpamayo 代码库结构分析报告

## 一、项目目录结构

```
/data/Zhengyang/alpamayo/
├── notebooks/
│   ├── inference.ipynb           # Physical AI AV 数据推理 demo
│   ├── inference_nuscenes.ipynb   # nuScenes 数据推理 demo
│   ├── explore_scenes.ipynb      # 场景浏览与 clip 选择
│   └── clip_ids.parquet          # 可用 clip_id 列表
├── src/
│   └── alpamayo_r1/
│       ├── __init__.py
│       ├── config.py              # AlpamayoR1Config 配置
│       ├── helper.py              # create_message, get_processor, to_device
│       ├── load_physical_aiavdataset.py   # Physical AI AV 数据加载
│       ├── load_nuscenes.py       # nuScenes 数据加载（替代 Physical AI）
│       ├── test_inference.py      # 命令行推理脚本
│       ├── action_space/          # 动作空间（轨迹 → 动作 / 动作 → 轨迹）
│       │   ├── action_space.py
│       │   ├── unicycle_accel_curvature.py
│       │   ├── discrete_action_space.py
│       │   └── utils.py
│       ├── diffusion/             # 扩散模型
│       │   ├── base.py
│       │   └── flow_matching.py
│       ├── geometry/
│       │   └── rotation.py
│       └── models/
│           ├── base_model.py      # ReasoningVLA / Qwen3-VL 基类
│           ├── alpamayo_r1.py     # AlpamayoR1 模型
│           ├── delta_tokenizer.py  # 轨迹 tokenizer
│           ├── action_in_proj.py  # 动作投影
│           └── token_utils.py     # 生成控制
├── pyproject.toml
├── uv.lock
└── README.md
```

---

## 二、模型架构

### 2.1 模型类型

- **Alpamayo-R1**：NVIDIA 的 Vision-Language-Action (VLA) 模型，用于自动驾驶
- 论文：*Alpamayo-R1: Bridging Reasoning and Action Prediction for Generalizable Autonomous Driving in the Long Tail* (arXiv:2511.00088)

### 2.2 架构组成

| 组件 | 实现 | 说明 |
|------|------|------|
| **VLM Backbone** | Qwen3VLForConditionalGeneration | 基于 Qwen3-VL-8B-Instruct（训练时）/ 2B（processor） |
| **Expert Model** | Llama 风格 LLM | 仅 transformer 层，无 `embed_tokens` |
| **Trajectory Tokenizer** | DeltaTrajectoryTokenizer | 历史/未来轨迹编码为离散 token |
| **Action Space** | UnicycleAccelCurvature 等 | 轨迹 ↔ 动作空间转换 |
| **Diffusion** | Flow Matching | 条件扩散生成未来动作 |

### 2.3 模型输入

1. **多相机图像**：`(N_cameras, num_frames, 3, H, W)` → 展平为 `(16, 3, H, W)` 传给 VLM
2. **自车历史轨迹**：`ego_history_xyz (1,1,16,3)`，`ego_history_rot (1,1,16,3,3)` → 通过 DeltaTrajectoryTokenizer 编码为 48 个 token，注入 `tokenized_data`
3. **文本 prompt**：通过 `helper.create_message()` 构造 chat 消息，含系统、用户（图像 + 文本）、助手占位符

### 2.4 模型输出

- **Chain-of-Causation (CoC)** 推理文本
- **预测轨迹**：64 个未来时间步（6.4s @ 10Hz），每步 3D 位置和旋转矩阵

---

## 三、相机配置

### 3.1 Physical AI AV 数据集（原始）

模型默认使用 **4 路相机**，索引与 FOV：

| 索引 | 摄像头名 | FOV |
|------|----------|-----|
| 0 | CAMERA_CROSS_LEFT_120FOV | 120° |
| 1 | CAMERA_FRONT_WIDE_120FOV | 120° |
| 2 | CAMERA_CROSS_RIGHT_120FOV | 120° |
| 6 | CAMERA_FRONT_TELE_30FOV | 30° |

完整物理名称与索引映射（`load_physical_aiavdataset.py` 第 80–88 行）：

```python
camera_name_to_index = {
    "camera_cross_left_120fov": 0,
    "camera_front_wide_120fov": 1,
    "camera_cross_right_120fov": 2,
    "camera_rear_left_70fov": 3,
    "camera_rear_tele_30fov": 4,
    "camera_rear_right_70fov": 5,
    "camera_front_tele_30fov": 6,
}
```

### 3.2 nuScenes 映射

`load_nuscenes.py` 中通过 `NUSCENES_CAMERA_MAPPING` 将 nuScenes 相机映射到上述索引：

```python
NUSCENES_CAMERA_MAPPING = [
    ("CAM_FRONT_LEFT", 0),   # ~ CAMERA_CROSS_LEFT_120FOV
    ("CAM_FRONT", 1),        # ~ CAMERA_FRONT_WIDE_120FOV
    ("CAM_FRONT_RIGHT", 2),  # ~ CAMERA_CROSS_RIGHT_120FOV
    ("CAM_FRONT", 6),        # ~ CAMERA_FRONT_TELE_30FOV（无 30° 对应，复用 CAM_FRONT）
]
```

注意：nuScenes 没有 30° 长焦，只能用 CAM_FRONT 近似替代。

### 3.3 帧与时序

- 每相机 **4 帧**，时间戳：`[t0-0.3s, t0-0.2s, t0-0.1s, t0]`，相当于 10Hz
- 总图像：4 相机 × 4 帧 = **16 张**
- `camera_indices` 排序后为 `[0, 1, 2, 6]`，保证相机顺序稳定

---

## 四、数据 pipeline

### 4.1 Physical AI AV 流程

```
load_physical_aiavdataset(clip_id, t0_us, ...)
    ↓
1. 获取 EGOMOTION 特征，采样 history/future 时间戳
2. 计算 ego 位姿并变换到 t0 局部坐标系
3. 对 4 个 camera_features 循环：
   - 调用 avdi.get_clip_feature(clip_id, cam_feature)
   - camera.decode_images_from_timestamps(image_timestamps) → (num_frames, H, W, 3)
   - rearrange "t h w c -> t c h w"
4. 按 camera_indices 排序
5. 返回 dict：image_frames, camera_indices, ego_history_xyz/rot, ego_future_xyz/rot 等
```

### 4.2 nuScenes 流程

```
load_nuscenes(scene_name, t0_us, ndi, ...)
    ↓
1. NuScenesDataInterface：读取 JSON 元数据，建索引
2. 从 LIDAR_TOP 链表收集 ego_pose，做插值
3. 计算 history/future 位姿并转到 t0 局部系
4. 按 NUSCENES_CAMERA_MAPPING 遍历：
   - ndi.find_nearest_camera_images(scene_token, channel, img_ts)
   - PIL.Image.open + rearrange → (T, 3, H, W)
5. 按 camera_indices 排序，输出与 load_physical_aiavdataset 相同的 dict
```

### 4.3 推理流程（数据 → 模型）

```
data = load_* (physical_aiavdataset 或 load_nuscenes)
    ↓
messages = helper.create_message(data["image_frames"].flatten(0, 1))
    # image_frames: (4, 4, 3, H, W) → flatten → (16, 3, H, W)
    ↓
inputs = processor.apply_chat_template(messages, ...)
    # 生成 input_ids 和 pixel_values（图像由 processor 处理）
    ↓
model_inputs = {
    "tokenized_data": inputs,
    "ego_history_xyz": data["ego_history_xyz"],
    "ego_history_rot": data["ego_history_rot"],
}
    ↓
pred_xyz, pred_rot, extra = model.sample_trajectories_from_data_with_vlm_rollout(...)
```

### 4.4 图像预处理

- **Processor**：`helper.get_processor()` 使用 `Qwen/Qwen3-VL-2B-Instruct` 的 AutoProcessor
- **像素范围**：`min_pixels=163840`，`max_pixels=196608`
- 图像通过 `processor.apply_chat_template()` 变为 `pixel_values`，与文本一起作为 VLM 输入
- 无额外 crop/resize 逻辑，由 Qwen3-VL processor 统一处理

---

## 五、相关代码位置速查

| 功能 | 文件 | 行号/说明 |
|------|------|-----------|
| 默认 4 相机配置 | `load_physical_aiavdataset.py` | 74–88 |
| 相机名到索引 | `load_physical_aiavdataset.py` | 80–88 |
| nuScenes 相机映射 | `load_nuscenes.py` | 19–24 |
| 消息构造（含图像） | `helper.py` | 28–67 |
| 图像展平进模型 | `test_inference.py`, 各 notebook | `data["image_frames"].flatten(0, 1)` |
| 模型前向与采样 | `models/alpamayo_r1.py` | 196–401 |
| VLM  backbone 初始化 | `models/base_model.py` | 366–380 |
| 轨迹 token 注入 | `models/base_model.py` | `fuse_traj_tokens` |

---

## 六、要点小结

- **4 路相机**：左交叉 120°、前广角 120°、右交叉 120°、前长焦 30°
- **前端视角为主**：前广角 + 前长焦 + 左右交叉，覆盖前向和两侧
- **时序**：每相机 4 帧、10Hz，共 16 张图
- **数据格式统一**：Physical AI AV 和 nuScenes 的 loader 都输出相同的 `image_frames`、`camera_indices` 等
- **30° FOV**：在 nuScenes 中无直接对应，用 CAM_FRONT 近似；与 Physical AI 的 FOV 差异会影响性能

---
